briefs.title: BC Municipalities Exempt from Provincial Highway Approval When Rezoning for More Housing
briefs.summary: BC updated rules so that local governments don't need provincial approval to rezone (change land-use rules) for small-scale multi-family and housing-needs housing — unless the land needs direct access to a controlled-access highway (like a freeway). This exemption runs until January 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: If your property is near a freeway or controlled-access highway, check with your municipality before relying on any new zoning changes.

key_dates.label: BC housing zoning approval exemption expires — provincial minister approval may be required again
